goal: 修复 Codex linked worktree 无法复用 Mainline actor identity

summary.title: Share Mainline identity across linked worktrees
summary.what: Moved actor identity lookup from strictly worktree-local .ml-cache to a clone-local model: current worktree identity still wins, git common-dir identity is shared across linked worktrees, and existing main-worktree .ml-cache identity remains a compatibility fallback. Identity writes now persist both the worktree-local and shared clone-local copies.
summary.why: Codex creates linked worktrees that contain the committed .mainline config but not the original worktree's gitignored .ml-cache/identity.json, so Mainline read surfaces worked but write paths could not pass the identity gate.
summary.user_goal: 修复 Codex linked worktree 无法复用 Mainline actor identity

decisions.point: Where to store reusable actor identity
decisions.chose: Use git common dir mainline/identity.json as the clone-local shared identity location.
decisions.rationale: The git common dir is untracked, local to the clone, and shared by linked worktrees.

decisions.chose: Keep drafts, views, hook state, and other .ml-cache data in each worktree.
decisions.rationale: Those files are branch/worktree-sensitive and should not be shared across parallel agent work.

decisions.chose: Read the main worktree's legacy .ml-cache/identity.json when no shared identity exists yet.
decisions.rationale: Existing clones do not need a manual migration before linked worktrees start working.

rejected.alternative: Require every Codex worktree to run mainline init
rejected.reason: That creates unnecessary new actor identities for the same physical clone.

rejected.alternative: Move all .ml-cache state into git common dir
rejected.reason: Drafts and view caches are branch/worktree-sensitive and should stay isolated.

risks: If two humans intentionally share one physical clone with linked worktrees, they will now share one actor identity unless they override the worktree-local identity file.

architectural_claims: Actor identity is clone-local and may be shared across linked worktrees.
Drafts and cache state remain worktree-local under .ml-cache.
behavioral_changes: Linked worktrees can pass Mainline identity checks without rerunning init when the original clone already has an identity.
New identity writes create a shared git-common-dir copy for future linked worktrees.
Existing clones with only legacy main-worktree .ml-cache identity continue to work from linked worktrees.

data_model_changes.name: clone-local identity file
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Existing main-worktree .ml-cache identity is read as a fallback; future writes populate the shared location.

security_implications: Actor identity remains local to the clone and is not committed to the repository.
migration_notes: No manual migration required; existing main-worktree identity is read as a fallback and future writes populate the shared location.
